task_completion.description: Load the CT Cranium DICOM series into InVesalius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 application window is open and displays axial, coronal, and sagittal CT slices of a human head/skull.
A colored overlay (segmentation mask) is visible on the 2D CT slices, specifically highlighting the high-density bone structures.
A 3D rendered model of the skull is visible in the 3D visualization pane of the InVesalius interface.

task_completion.description: Document at least three linear cranial measurements
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three linear measurement annotations (lines with numerical distance values) are drawn across the skull structures in the 2D slice views using the software's measurement tool.

task_completion.description: Capture 3D screenshots from anterior, left lateral, and superior orientations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D skull model is oriented to face forward (anterior), sideways (left lateral), and from above (superior) in the 3D pane, with evidence of saving these views (e.g., export image dialogs for anterior_view.png, lateral_view.png, superior_view.png).

task_completion.description: Save the complete project as an .inv3 file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project' dialog box is visible, showing the filename 'skull_study.inv3' being saved into the '/home/ga/Documents/surgical_views' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the InVesalius menus, toolbars, and dialogs to create masks, 3D surfaces, and measurements.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to mock the output files
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'touch' or 'cp' being used to create dummy .inv3 or .png files with the required names.

integrity.description: Measurements were created using the built-in tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement lines and text should match the native UI style of InVesalius 3, rather than being drawn over the screenshot using an external image editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius 3 to process the 'ct_cranium/0051' DICOM series. The agent must create a bone mask and 3D surface, then take 3D screenshots from anterior, left lateral, and superior views saved as 'anterior_view.png', 'lateral_view.png', and 'superior_view.png' in '/home/ga/Documents/surgical_views/'. The agent must also use the measurement tool to create at least 3 linear measurements (each >= 30.0 mm) on the 2D slices. Finally, the project must be saved as 'skull_study.inv3' in the same directory.
